workflow.name: Optimize classroom schedules and resolve conflicts with GPT-4o and Google Calendar
workflow.description:
## How It Works
This workflow automates competitive intelligence gathering and market analysis for businesses needing real-time insights on competitors, industry trends, and market positioning. Designed for marketing teams, strategy analysts, and business development professionals, it solves the time-intensive challenge of manually monitoring competitor activities across multiple channels. The system schedules regular data collection, fetches competitor information from various sources, employs multiple AI agents (OpenAI for analysis, sentiment evaluation, and report generation) to process data, validates outputs through structured parsing, and delivers comprehensive reports via email. By automating data aggregation, sentiment analysis, and insight generation, organizations gain actionable intelligence faster, identify market opportunities proactively, and maintain competitive advantage through continuous monitoring—essential for dynamic markets where timing determines success.

## Setup Steps
1. Connect **Schedule Trigger** (set monitoring frequency: daily/weekly)
2. Configure **Fetch Data** node with competitor website URLs/APIs
3. Add **OpenAI API keys** to all AI agent nodes 
4. Link **Google Sheets** credentials for storing historical analysis data
5. Configure **Gmail** node with SMTP credentials for report distribution
6. Set up **Slack/Discord** webhooks for instant critical alert notifications

## Prerequisites
OpenAI API account (GPT-4 recommended), competitor data sources/APIs
## Use Cases
SaaS competitor feature tracking, retail pricing intelligence
## Customization
Modify AI prompts for industry-specific metrics, adjust sentiment thresholds for alert triggers
## Benefits
Reduces research time by 85%, provides 24/7 competitor monitoring, eliminates manual data aggregation

workflow.name: AI-Driven Classroom Scheduling and Conflict Resolution System

options.systemMessage:
You are a Resource Analysis Agent specialized in evaluating classroom scheduling constraints.

Your task is to:
1. Analyze classroom availability, capacity, and equipment against timetable requests
2. Evaluate faculty workload constraints including max hours per day and preferred time slots
3. Identify equipment mismatches between course requirements and classroom capabilities
4. Calculate classroom utilization rates and identify over/under-utilized spaces
5. Detect scheduling conflicts including:
   - Double-booked classrooms
   - Faculty teaching multiple classes simultaneously
   - Insufficient break time between classes
   - Capacity violations (students exceeding room capacity)
   - Equipment unavailability
6. Assess overall scheduling feasibility
7. Flag critical issues that require immediate attention
8. Return structured analysis with detailed breakdowns

Consider:
- Classroom capacity must exceed required capacity by at least 10% for safety
- Faculty cannot exceed their maximum daily hours
- Minimum break time between classes must be respected
- Equipment requirements must be fully met
- Building proximity for back-to-back faculty classes

Provide comprehensive resource analysis in the defined JSON structure.

parameters.text:
=Resource Analysis Results: {{ JSON.stringify($json.output) }}

Original Requests: {{ JSON.stringify($('Prepare Scheduling Data').first().json.timetableRequests) }}

options.systemMessage:
You are an Operations Agent specialized in orchestrating classroom scheduling and conflict resolution.

Your task is to:
1. Review the resource analysis from the Resource Analysis Agent
2. Generate optimal scheduling recommendations that:
   - Maximize classroom utilization
   - Respect all faculty constraints
   - Meet equipment requirements
   - Minimize conflicts
   - Balance workload across time slots
3. Identify and classify conflicts by severity:
   - CRITICAL: Safety violations, double bookings, impossible constraints
   - HIGH: Significant capacity issues, major equipment gaps
   - MEDIUM: Suboptimal scheduling, minor constraint violations
   - LOW: Preference mismatches, efficiency improvements
4. Provide alternative scheduling options for conflicted courses
5. Determine if escalation to human decision-makers is required
6. Generate actionable recommendations with specific resolution steps
7. Assess overall scheduling status (OPTIMAL, ACCEPTABLE, PROBLEMATIC, CRITICAL)

Escalation criteria:
- Any CRITICAL severity conflicts
- More than 3 HIGH severity conflicts
- Impossible to schedule all courses within constraints
- Faculty workload violations that cannot be resolved

Provide comprehensive scheduling recommendations in the defined JSON structure with clear action items.

parameters.html:
=

Classroom Scheduling Recommendations

Overall Status: {{ $json.output.schedulingRecommendations.overallStatus }}

Optimal Schedule

{{ $json.output.schedulingRecommendations.optimalSchedule.map(s => ` `).join("") }}
Course ID Classroom Faculty Time Slot Duration (min) Confidence
${s.courseId} ${s.classroomId} ${s.facultyId} ${s.timeSlot} ${s.duration} ${(s.confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%
{{ $json.output.schedulingRecommendations.conflicts.length > 0 ? `

Conflicts Detected (${$json.output.schedulingRecommendations.conflicts.length})

${$json.output.schedulingRecommendations.conflicts.map(c => `
[${c.severity}] ${c.type}
${c.description}
Affected Courses: ${c.affectedCourses.join(", ")}
Resolution Options:
    ${c.resolutionOptions.map(opt => `
  • ${opt}
    • `).join("")}
`).join("")}` : "

No conflicts detected.

" }}

Action Items

{{ $json.output.schedulingRecommendations.actionItems.map((item, i) => `
${i+1}. ${item}
`).join("") }}

Generated: {{ $now.toISO() }}
parameters.subject: =Classroom Scheduling Recommendations - {{ $json.output.schedulingRecommendations.overallStatus }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a Schedule Optimization specialist.

Your task is to:
1. Analyze the provided schedule and identify optimization opportunities
2. Apply advanced scheduling algorithms to maximize efficiency
3. Consider historical patterns and preferences
4. Minimize conflicts while respecting all constraints
5. Calculate optimization scores for each proposed change
6. Return optimized schedule with efficiency metrics

parameters.toolDescription: Optimizes classroom schedules using advanced algorithms and historical data to maximize efficiency and minimize conflicts

parameters.description: Calculates weighted conflict scores based on severity, affected courses, and complexity to prioritize resolution efforts

options.systemMessage:
You are the Master Orchestrator Agent coordinating complex classroom scheduling decisions.

Your task is to:
1. Synthesize inputs from Resource Analysis, Historical Patterns, and Operations recommendations
2. Use the Schedule Optimization Agent Tool to generate optimized schedules
3. Use the Conflict Score Calculator Tool to quantify conflict severity
4. Use the Google Calendar Tool to check existing calendar events and availability
5. Determine if conflicts can be auto-resolved based on:
   - Conflict score below threshold
   - High confidence in optimization (>85%)
   - No CRITICAL severity conflicts
   - Calendar availability confirmed
6. Provide comprehensive decision with reasoning
7. Recommend specific actions for both auto-resolution and manual escalation paths

Decision criteria for auto-resolution:
- Total conflict score < 15
- Optimization confidence > 85%
- No CRITICAL conflicts
- All affected time slots available in calendar
- Historical success rate for similar scenarios > 75%

Return structured decision with confidence scores and detailed reasoning.

## Setup Steps
1. Connect **Schedule Trigger** (set monitoring frequency: daily/weekly)
2. Configure **Fetch Data** node with competitor website URLs/APIs
3. Add **OpenAI API keys** to all AI agent nodes 
4. Link **Google Sheets** credentials for storing historical analysis data
5. Configure **Gmail** node with SMTP credentials for report distribution
6. Set up **Slack/Discord** webhooks for instant critical alert notifications

## Prerequisites
OpenAI API account (GPT-4 recommended), competitor data sources/APIs
## Use Cases
SaaS competitor feature tracking, retail pricing intelligence
## Customization
Modify AI prompts for industry-specific metrics, adjust sentiment thresholds for alert triggers
## Benefits
Reduces research time by 85%, provides 24/7 competitor monitoring, eliminates manual data aggregation

## How It Works
This workflow automates competitive intelligence gathering and market analysis for businesses needing real-time insights on competitors, industry trends, and market positioning. Designed for marketing teams, strategy analysts, and business development professionals, it solves the time-intensive challenge of manually monitoring competitor activities across multiple channels. The system schedules regular data collection, fetches competitor information from various sources, employs multiple AI agents (OpenAI for analysis, sentiment evaluation, and report generation) to process data, validates outputs through structured parsing, and delivers comprehensive reports via email. By automating data aggregation, sentiment analysis, and insight generation, organizations gain actionable intelligence faster, identify market opportunities proactively, and maintain competitive advantage through continuous monitoring—essential for dynamic markets where timing determines success.

## Report Generation & Distribution
**Why**: Delivers actionable intelligence to decision-makers instantly for rapid strategic pivots

## Multi-Agent AI Analysis
**Why**: Delivers expert-level analysis across multiple dimensions simultaneously, uncovering hidden patterns humans miss

## Automated Data Collection
**Why**: Schedule trigger initiates competitor data fetching at defined intervals to ensure continuous market monitoring without manual intervention, capturing time-sensitive competitive moves

## Intelligent Routing & Validation
**Why**: Ensures data quality and prioritizes critical insights requiring immediate strategic response

user.name: Cheng Siong Chin
user.bio: Dr. Cheng Siong CHIN is an n8n workflow creator specializing in AI-powered automation, agent orchestration, and intelligent system integrations. He designs and builds end-to-end workflows that combine LLMs, APIs, and data pipelines to streamline complex processes and deliver production-ready automation solutions. Contact me to discuss custom AI workflows and agent architectures.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
